info.name: Decentro Fabric & Flow APIs
info.description:
Our currently live collection of API modules and sub-modules exposed to you for any kind of financial integration as below.  
Just choose your APIs, plug in your credentials, and test it out with your system! Feel free to sign up in a few seconds here on the [Decentro website](http://bit.ly/Decentro-Signup), if you haven't already received your staging or production credentials.

It's that easy, as it should be. Welcome to the future.

---

## Terms of Use

Thank you for using our APIs, and any linked software (collectively, “APIs”). By accessing or using our APIs, you are agreeing to our terms as written [here](https://decentro.tech/terms/).

## API Status

Available at [Decentro API Status](http://status.decentro.tech/), it reports uptime, maintenance windows and incidents for our API environments.

## REST clients

The best way to get started with our APIs is to click the 'Run in Postman' button above. This will automatically open up Postman (on browser or desktop) for you and import our full API collection there. All REST clients like [Postman](https://www.postman.com/) (which is a free tool) can easily be used to run and test our API requests.

## Support/Questions?

In case of any questions, feel free to write to us as below -

- Support around APIs or transactions - [support@decentro.tech](https://mailto:support@decentro.tech)
    
- Business and pricing related - [hello@decentro.tech](https://mailto:hello@decentro.tech)
    

## API Base URLs (India)

- Sandbox - [https://in.staging.decentro.tech](https://in.staging.decentro.tech)
    
- Production - [https://in.decentro.tech](https://in.decentro.tech)
    

## Missing/no keys for testing?

Feel free to sign up in a few seconds here on the [Decentro website](http://bit.ly/Decentro-Signup) if you don't have your credentials yet. Our team will give you a quick call, but remember to use your company's (or employer's) official email ID only.

request.description:
Generate and fetch credit report using detailed parameters or just using Mobile number and Name.  
Basis the request payload, Decentro hits the relevant report in the backend accordingly.

**Please note Decentro recommends using all the parameters for higher accuracy**.

Also, Decentro would recommend using its [KYC validation API](https://docs.decentro.tech/#ae6c5126-2c27-47b6-9ca4-575261ea7728) to check the PAN number and other ID details to verify if only mobile number and name are used to pull the report.

| Parameter | Description | Optional (O)/ Mandatory (M)/ Conditionally Mandatory (CM) | Datatype |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| name | Full name of the person | M | string |
| consent |  | M | Boolean (true/false) |
| consent_purpose |  | M | String (Min 20 chars and Max 50 Chars) |
| date_of_birth | Date of birth of the person. Accepted format is `YYYY-MM-DD` | O | string |
| address | Current address of the customer | O | string |
| address_type | Type of address. Refer below for allowed types and respective code | O | string |
| pincode | Pincode of the current address | O | string |
| mobile | Mobile number of the person | M | string |
| inquiry_purpose | Reason for inquiry. Refer below table for allowed types and respective code | M | string |
| document_type | The type of ID required for validation. Allowed values are \[AADHAAR / DRIVING_LICENSE / PAN / PASSPORT/ VOTERID\] | O | string |
| document_id | The national ID number that is present on the above `document_type`. | O | string |

ADDRESS TYPE

| Address Type | Code |
| --- | --- |
| Home | H |
| Office | O |
| Others | X |

INQUIRY PURPOSE

| Inquiry Purpose | Code |
| --- | --- |
| BusinessLoan | BL |
| ConsumerLoan | CL |
| CreditCard | CC |
| GoldLoan | GL |
| HomeLoan | HL |
| PersonalLoan | PL |

request.description:
## Request Specification

### Request Body Parameters

| Parameter | Data Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `mobile` | String | Yes | Customer's 10-digit mobile number. Must start with 6, 7, 8, or 9. |
| `name` | String | Yes | Customer's full name as registered with credit bureau. |

### Example Request

``` json
{
    "mobile": "9999999999",
    "name": "John Doe"
}

 ```

## Response Specification

### Success Response Structure

| Key | Data Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `decentroTxnId` | String | Unique transaction identifier for the API call. Used for debugging and tracking. |
| `status` | String | Overall status of the API call. Possible values: "SUCCESS", "FAILURE". |
| `responseCode` | String | Specific code indicating the result of the operation. Format: "S" prefix for success, "E" prefix for errors. |
| `message` | String | Human-readable description of the API call result. |
| `data` | Object | Contains the credit score information when available. Only present in successful score retrievals. |
| `data.scoreDetails` | Array | List of credit score objects. May contain multiple score types. |
| `scoreDetails[].type` | String | Code identifying the scoring model used (e.g., "ERS"). |
| `scoreDetails[].version` | String | Version of the scoring model (e.g., "4.0"). |
| `scoreDetails[].name` | String | Display name of the scoring model (e.g., "ERS4.0"). |
| `scoreDetails[].value` | String | The actual credit score value. |
| `scoringElements` | Array | Factors that influenced the credit score. |
| `scoringElements[].type` | String | Type of scoring element (e.g., "RES" for reason). |
| `scoringElements[].seq` | String | Sequence number indicating importance of the factor. |
| `scoringElements[].code` | String | Code identifying the specific factor. |
| `scoringElements[].description` | String | Human-readable description of the factor. |
| `responseKey` | String | Standardized key for the response type. Useful for programmatic handling. |

### Response Scenarios

#### 1\. Successful Credit Score Retrieval

| HTTP Status | 200 OK |
| --- | --- |
| `status` | "SUCCESS" |
| `responseCode` | "S00000" |
| `message` | "Credit score fetched successfully." |
| `responseKey` | "success_credit_score" |
| Description | The API successfully retrieved the customer's credit score from Equifax. |

``` json
{
    "decentroTxnId": "1C03B0450BEA436B8A273F0CEB39D104",
    "status": "SUCCESS",
    "responseCode": "S00000",
    "message": "Credit score fetched successfully.",
    "data": {
        "scoreDetails": [
            {
                "type": "ERS",
                "version": "4.0",
                "name": "ERS4.0",
                "value": "816",
                "scoringElements": [
                    {
                        "type": "RES",
                        "seq": "1",
                        "code": "703",
                        "description": "Total Utilization"
                    },
                    {
                        "type": "RES",
                        "seq": "2",
                        "code": "706",
                        "description": "Number of Inquiries"
                    },
                    {
                        "type": "RES",
                        "seq": "3",
                        "code": "704",
                        "description": "Credit Card Utilization"
                    }
                ]
            }
        ]
    },
    "responseKey": "success_credit_score"
}

 ```

#### 2\. Customer Not Found

| HTTP Status | 200 OK |
| --- | --- |
| `status` | "SUCCESS" |
| `responseCode` | "E00058" |
| `message` | "Credit score not found." |
| `responseKey` | "error_credits_score_not_found" |
| Description | The API call was successful, but no credit records were found for the provided customer details. This indicates the customer may not have a credit history with Equifax. |

``` json
{
    "decentroTxnId": "E5AC0C5E6D6D42EA9E28B43D08A512C8",
    "status": "SUCCESS",
    "responseCode": "E00058",
    "message": "Credit score not found.",
    "responseKey": "error_credits_score_not_found"
}

 ```

#### 3\. Invalid Mobile Number

| HTTP Status | 400 Bad Request |
| --- | --- |
| `status` | "FAILURE" |
| `responseCode` | "E00009" |
| `message` | "Mobile is not valid. Please provide a valid mobile containing 10 digits starting with 6, 7, 8 or 9." |
| `responseKey` | "error_invalid_mobile" |
| Description | The provided mobile number does not meet validation requirements. The request was rejected before attempting to retrieve credit information. |

``` json
{
    "decentroTxnId": "87EA90D3D33F4F27B1368EA604855638",
    "status": "FAILURE",
    "responseCode": "E00009",
    "message": "Mobile is not valid. Please provide a valid mobile containing 10 digits starting with 6, 7, 8 or 9.",
    "responseKey": "error_invalid_mobile"
}

 ```

## Response Code Reference

| Response Key | HTTP Status | Message |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `success_credit_score` | 200 | Credit score fetched successfully. |
| `error_credits_score_not_found` | 200 | Credit score not found. |
| `error_invalid_mobile` | 400 | Mobile is not valid. Please provide a valid mobile containing 10 digits starting with 6, 7, 8 or 9. |

## Implementation Guidelines

### Error Handling

- Always check the `status` and `responseCode` fields to determine the result of the API call
    
- Note that an HTTP 200 response may still contain error information (e.g., customer not found)
    
- Implement appropriate retry logic for temporary failures
    

### Data Processing

- Credit score values should be treated as strings in processing logic
    
- The `scoringElements` array is ordered by importance (seq field)
    
- The top three scoring elements represent the most significant factors affecting the credit score
    

### Security Considerations

- Implement proper authentication before making API calls
    
- Always transmit customer information over secure channels
    
- Obtain appropriate consent before retrieving credit information

item.description:
This module provides you with a whole array of APIs to serve multiple financial services and fetch data for an organization or individual from various data sources in a streamlined manner. 

Some of the key things that you can do here - 
- Fetch the credit report from any of our partner credit bureaus
- Fetch the GST Returns data from the government databases 
and more.

item.name: Accounts
Virtual Accounts
Account v2

header.description: Decentro assigned client_id for the company
Decentro assigned client_secret for the company
Decentro assigned banking module_secret for the company

request.description:
This API allows you to create a virtual account with single/multi provider feature for a consumer or business in real-time. This functions exactly like a regular bank account with the exception of cash withdrawals. You can also choose the underlying bank provider if you have a particular preference. | Parameter | Description | Optional (O)/ Mandatory (M)/ Conditionally mandatory (CM) | Datatype | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | bank_codes | Provider Bank 4 digit code | M | String | | name | Customer name to be put against the account and linked with UPI - ID | M | String | | pan | PAN of the customer / merchant | CM; Mandatory when upi_onboarding is set to true | String | | email | Contact email address of the customer/ merchant | O | String | | mobile | Mobile of the entity | M | String | | address | Physical address of the entity | O | String | | kyc_verified | 0 / 1 depending on the status of the customer / merchant at your end.Please note that the onus of KYC lies on you | M | Integer: 0 / 1 | | kyc_check_decentro | 0 / 1 depending on the status of the KYC check requirement from our end(if used).Default value is 0 | M | Integer: 0 / 1 | | minimum_balance | Minimum balance to be maintained | O | String, but should be a parseable positive Float / Integer value | | transaction_limit | Maximum transaction amount limit | O | String, but should be a parseable positive Float / Integer value | | customer_id | Unique customer ID to be saved against the customer | M | String | | virtual_account_balance_settlement | Flag to enable/disable the virtual account for balance settlement.For VA's with disabled flag will not be included in balance settlement API. | O | String
(Accepted values - enabled OR disabled) | | custom_vpa | Custom name (lowercase, if not will be converted to lowercase) that is created as a prefix of upi-id handle | O | Min length 3 and max length 100 | | upi_onboarding | 1 if sub - merchant onboarding is required | O | Boolean: true / false | | state_code | State of the entity. kindly use [/state API](https://docs.decentro.tech/#381eb4e9-b5ef-4d82-9bc7-342f1abdff37) to get respective state code. | CM; Mandatory when upi_onboarding is set to 1 | Integer | | city | City of the entity | CM; Mandatory when upi_onboarding is set to 1 | String | | pincode | Pincode of the entity | CM; Mandatory when upi_onboarding is set to 1 | Integer | | upi_onboarding_details | A complex object containing the details for enabling UPI transaction mode | CM; Mandatory when upi_onboarding is set to 1 | Complex | | merchant_category_code | Category code of the customer / merchant. Kindly use [/core_banking/upi/merchant_category API](https://docs.decentro.tech/#ea4f7029-6e73-4090-881f-ab15d99e9f45) to get respective category code | CM based on the existence of the parameter upi_onboarding_details | String | | merchant_business_type | Business Type of the customer / merchant. Kindly use [/core_banking/upi/business_type API](https://docs.decentro.tech/#1fda728e-fe20-4efd-b1ef-f3356867d910) to get respective business type | CM based on the existence of the parameter upi_onboarding_details | String | | transaction_count_limit_per_day | Maximum number of transactions that can be done in a day via UPI | CM based on the existence of the parameter upi_onboarding_details | Integer | | transaction_amount_limit_per_day | Maximum amount that can be transferred in a day via UPI and kindly refer below for provider specific limits | CM based on the existence of the parameter upi_onboarding_details | Integer | | transaction_amount_limit_per_transaction | Maximum amount that can be transferred in a single transaction and this amount should be less than `transaction_amount_limit_per_day` | CM based on the existence of the parameter upi_onboarding_details | Integer | | master_account | A JSON object containing the details for linked account on top of which the virtual account has to be created. If empty, Decentro's default account will be used. Enabled only for ICICI as a banking provider. | CM, Mandatory when company is onboarded more than one business accounts.(Mandatory when 'master_account_alias' is not present) | JSON | | number | Account number for master account | CM based on the existence of the parameter master_account | String | | ifsc | Account IFSC for master account | CM based on the existence of the parameter master_account | String | | master_account_alias | Alias provided by Decentro team | CM, Mandatory when company is onboarded more than one business accounts.(Mandatory when 'master_account' JSON is not present) | String | 𝗨𝗣𝗜 𝗧𝗥𝗔𝗡𝗦𝗔𝗖𝗧𝗜𝗢𝗡 𝗟𝗜𝗠𝗜𝗧𝗦 | Bank | Upper Limit Per Transaction | Per Day Transaction Limit | | --- | --- | --- | | Any Bank | Rs.1, 00, 000 | Rs.1, 00, 000 | *implies that the marked parameters are currently not being considered and are slated to be in the future releases

Here you can - 
- Create virtual accounts for your customers/buyers/partners/sellers in real time with any of our partner banks.
- Manage/Update the virtual accounts in terms of limits and the personal details linked to each of those accounts.

item.description:
Decentro provides capabilities to fetch details about the virtual accounts created by the platform. Below are the capabilities supported by Decentro.

*   Fetch all the virtual accounts created for your customers/partners/businesses from your platform.
*   Extract the relevant account details like the linked UPI ID, QR code, permissible transaction types and more.
*   Fetch the latest balance of a virtual account
*   Fetch the statement for a specific time period for a virtual account
*   Fetch the consolidated statement containing statement of all the accounts for a combination of company and bank partner.

request.description:
Via this callback, it is possible to get realtime updates to the account balances.

Salient points:

1. 3 times maximum retry, post which the explicit Get account balance call has to be made for obtaining the current account balance.
2. It is mandatory to adhere to the response structure as specified in the examples.
3. It is recommended that you whitelist Decentro's originating IP.
4. It is recommended that you share your auth of preference with us, which will then be propagated as part of the callback request.
5. The `type` parameter can either be `credit` or `debit`.
6. The keys in the request will be in snake/ camel case based on the company configuration (defaulting to camel case if nothing opted). Response, however, has to be in snake case always.
7. The keys `payeeAccountNumber`, `payeeAccountIfsc`, `payeeVpa`, `payerAccountNumber`, `payerAccountIfsc`, `payerVpa`,`payerName`, `payerMobileNumber` will be present when the corresponding values are available, i.e. they are optional.

Scenario overview:

| Scenario      | Callback HTTP Status code |    Expected callback response|
| --- | --- | ----|
| Successful callback      | 200, 201     |  {"response_code": "CB_S00000"}|
| Unauthorized callback      | 401, 403     |  {"response_code": "CB_E00013"}|
| Failed callback      | Other 4xx     |  {"response_code": "CB_E00009"}|
| Failed callback due to internal server error     | 5xx     |  {"response_code": "CB_E00000"}|

item.description:
Decentro uses callbacks to intimate the platform about the change in the balance of a virtual account. This change can be because of an incoming transaction made by a customer or an outgoing transaction made from a virtual account.

Decentro can configure custom end points (URLs) that you can share with our team on [support@decentro.tech](mailto:support@decentro.tech) or with your assigned RM, you can receive callbacks for any changes in the balance for your virtual accounts, or status updates for any transaction.

**Overview**: Virtual accounts are basically a way to collect funds from individuals and businesses seamlessly and help in reconciliation.

Virtual accounts are setup on underlying business accounts, which can be current, escrow or nodal accounts.

Decentro has the capability to power Virtual accounts with its bank partners where the underlying account is owned by itself or the merchant's.

The underlying partner banks that we have currently are.

*   Yes Bank (Live)
*   ICICI Bank (Live)
*   Axis Bank (Live)
    

And more in pipeline.

[**Here's a detailed walkthrough on Virtual Accounts**](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=08mqXZn0Ni8)

**Use-cases**: Virtual accounts can be created for the below use-cases.

*   Collect funds from businesses through NEFT/IMPS/RTGS
*   Collect funds from individuals through IMPS or UPI (Collect Request/Payment Link/Push)
*   Payout to beneficiaries from the virtual account using any mode (NEFT/RTGS/IMPS/UPI)
    

**Product Flow**: Below is the recommended high level flow for using the virtual account stack.

*   [Create a virtual account](https://docs.decentro.tech/#e92377d4-5848-4b0a-8a0c-f57101758d0e) for a customer/partner/vendor
*   [Configure callbacks](https://docs.decentro.tech/#ae843df2-00ea-488d-8854-7472618a7671) for balance and transaction updates
*   [Validate bank account](https://docs.decentro.tech/#732f42e4-1039-475e-8291-a05d9f52472d) using our Penny drop service instantly
*   [Payout](https://docs.decentro.tech/#7b41103a-a1b8-4309-a27f-fe98e02eb4ce) to your beneficiaries using any of the supported modes
*   [Fetch the balance](https://docs.decentro.tech/#fcd452b9-3948-44c0-ad03-f16303ae1c9b) of the virtual account to transfer funds
*   [Fetch the statement](https://docs.decentro.tech/#f6291787-1f57-49bb-9940-abd40079ae74) to reconcile the transactions done against the virtual account
    

Decentro's [UPI & Payments stack](https://docs.decentro.tech/#b0a1daf7-1da3-4bf3-8a88-e2ac8a89244a) can be plugged into the virtual accounts setup for simple reconciliation, which is detailed in the folder.

Here you can find APIs around creation, linking, and management of bank accounts along with seamless and inter-operable money transfers with automated reconciliation.

This includes but is not limited to virtual/savings/business account creation, updation, management, money flow, collections, bank statement analysis and much more.

request.description:
Add / Update / Disable any beneficiary for sending money from the virtual account (on top of the underlying physical bank account)

| Parameter | Description | Optional (O)/ Mandatory (M)/ Conditionally mandatory (CM) | Datatype |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| action | Can be ADD/ VERIFY/ MODIFY/ DISABLE | M | String |
| name | Beneficiary name | M only if action is UPDATE | String |
| ifsc | Beneficiary bank IFSC code | M | String |
| sender_account_number | Account number against which the beneficiary is to be added. If not passed, beneficiary will be added to company's virtual account. | M | String |
| account_number | Beneficiary account number | M | String |
| upi_handle | UPI handle | M, if transfer type is UPI |  |
| beneficiary_code | Unique code assigned by you to the beneficiary | M only if action is not ADD | String |
| transfer_type | Can be NEFT/ IMPS/ RTGS/ UPI. If not specified for the ADD action, defaulted to OTHR. | O only if action is ADD | String |
| sender_mobile | Beneficiary mobile number | M only if action is ADD; O in case of all other action types | String |
| email | Email of beneficiary | O | String |

*   implies that the marked parameters are currently not being considered and are slated to be in the future releases

request.description:
This API can be used by you to check the money transfer transactions that were initiated/completed by your customers.

Two main parameters that are required -

*   reference_id ((mandatory if decentro_txn_id is not present) The request ID passed by you for this transaction when initiating it earlier.)
*   decentro_txn_id ((mandatory if reference_id is not present) The decentro transaction id that we responded with while providing the initiate payment response.)

item.description:
Decentro provides the capability to pay to bank accounts of customers, individuals or businesses using any of the supporting modes of transfers.

The platform is expected to [Add a Beneficiary](https://docs.decentro.tech/#fd50144e-38c3-4519-b25f-d664928beadb) to use the Nodal account for payouts. If this is not done, the transfer is rejected. **Note**: This is as per RBI guidelines.

The platform can [Add a Beneficiary](https://docs.decentro.tech/#fd50144e-38c3-4519-b25f-d664928beadb) to use any other account, but is recommended so that transfers to the same customer can done repeatedly without hassles.

Decentro recommends the platform to [Validate bank accounts](https://docs.decentro.tech/#732f42e4-1039-475e-8291-a05d9f52472d) before transacting to ensure the recipient is as per records.

request.description:
Send a payment collection request to a specific UPI ID (example - abcd@bank).

| Parameter | Description | Optional (O)/ Mandatory (M)/ Conditionally mandatory (CM) | Datatype |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| reference_id | A unique ID assigned by you for this transaction | M | String |
| payer_upi | UPI-ID (Virtual Payment Address) from where the money has to be requested | M | String |
| payee_account | Account number in which the money has to be received | M | String |
| amount | The transaction amount | M | Decimal (Float/Integer/Double) |
| purpose_message | The narration that you want to show as a message for the transfer.(Note: Purpose message must be at least 5 and not more than 50 characters) | M | String |
| expiry_time | Expiry time of this collection request in minutes. Default value is 30 minutes. (Note: Expiry time must be less than 45 days (i.e) 64800 mins) | O | Integer |

request.description:
Generate a payment link to send to a customer and/or a dynamic UPI QR code to show for a specific transaction.

| Parameter | Description | Optional (O)/ Mandatory (M)/ Conditionally mandatory (CM) | Datatype |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| reference\_id | A unique ID assigned by you for this transaction | M | String |
| payee\_account | Account number in which the amount has to be received | M | String |
| amount | The transaction amount | M | Decimal (Float/Integer/Double) |
| purpose\_message | The narration that you want to show as a message for the transfer. (Note: Purpose message must be at least 5 and not more than 50 characters) | M | String |
| generate\_qr | 0/1 depending on the choice to generate QR. 1 for generating QR and 0 for not generating QR | M | Integer |
| expiry\_time | Expiry time of this collection request in minutes. Default value is 30 minutes. (Note: Expiry time must be less than 45 days (i.e) 64800 mins) | O | Integer |
| customized\_qr\_with\_logo | 0/1 depending on whether you want to get a customised QR code | O | Integer |
| generate\_uri | 0/1 depending on the choice to generate UPI data URI to implement customized flow based on URI data | O | Integer |

We support multiple methods like UPI QR codes, payment links, as well as sending direct collection requests to the designated UPI ID.

item.description: This collection of APIs enables you to collect money via UPI (Unified Payments Interface) from your customers, or even enable your partner businesses/SMEs to do the same. We support multiple methods like UPI QR codes, payment links, as well as sending direct collection requests to the designated UPI ID.

item.name: Transaction Callbacks

request.description:
Via this callback, it is possible to get realtime updates to the initiated transactions' statuses.

Salient points:
1. 3 times maximum retry, post which the explicit Get status call has to be made for obtaining the transaction status.
2. It is mandatory to adhere to the response structure as specified in the examples.
3. It is recommended that you whitelist Decentro's originating IP.
4. It is recommended that you share your auth of preference with us, which will then be propagated as part of the callback request.
5. Callbacks will be triggered only in case of conclusive statuses (failure or success) and not while the transaction is pending.
6. `beneficiaryName` and `bankReferenceNumber` will be present only when Decentro's banking partners respond accordingly.
7. The keys in the request will be in snake/ camel case based on the company configuration (defaulting to camel case if nothing opted). Response, however, has to be in snake case always.

Scenario overview:

| Scenario      | Callback HTTP Status code |    Expected callback response|
| --- | --- | ----|
| Successful transaction, successful callback      | 200, 201     |  {"response_code": "CB_S00000"}|
| Successful transaction, unauthorized callback      | 401, 403     |  {"response_code": "CB_E00013"}|
| Successful transaction, failed callback      | Other 4xx     |  {"response_code": "CB_E00009"}|
| Successful transaction, failed callback due to internal server error     | 5xx     |  {"response_code": "CB_E00000"}|
| Failed transaction, successful callback      | 200, 201     |  {"response_code": "CB_S00000"}|
| Failed transaction, unauthorized callback      | 401, 403     |  {"response_code": "CB_E00013"}|
| Failed transaction, failed callback      | Other 4xx     |  {"response_code": "CB_E00009"}|
| Failed transaction, failed callback due to internal server error     | 5xx     |  {"response_code": "CB_E00000"}|

request.description:
Via this callback, it is possible for any company to accept or reject incoming transactions for their virtual accounts based on their custom criteria.

The incoming transactions will be accepted under the following scenarios:
- Company has not opted for pre-deposit checks.
- Company accepts the transaction.
- Company does not respond with the valid payload. (Refer examples)
- Company does not respond within the configured timeframe of 15 seconds.

The custom endpoints for different providers(if opted) need to be different.

response.name: Accept
Reject

item.description:
Decentro uses callbacks to intimate the platform about transactions, incoming or outgoing and irrespective of the mode of transfer. The callback will be posted on the end points provided by the platform.

Decentro can configure custom end points (URLs) that you can share with our team on [support@decentro.tech](mailto:support@decentro.tech) or with your assigned RM, you can receive callbacks for any changes in the balance for your virtual accounts, or status updates for any transaction.

request.description:
This API helps to enable a path (pageLink) so that your consumer can register a recurring payment with with Debit Card or Netbanking as a mode of payment. | Parameter | Description | Optional (O)/ Mandatory (M)/ Conditionally mandatory (CM) | Datatype | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | reference_id | A specific unique ID assigned by you for this transaction. If not kept unique, transaction status extraction could behave erratically. | M | String | | consumer | Consumer Details block | M | JSON | | name | Name of the consumer | M | String | | account_number | Account Number of the consumer | M | String | | account_type | Type of Account. Permissible values: SAVINGS, CURRENT, OTHER. | M | Fixed
(SAVINGS, CURRENT, OTHER) | | reference_number | Customer Reference Number | M | String | | bank_id | Bank Code of the account | M | Fixed (Bank Code as mentioned in supporting banks) | | email | Email ID of the consumer | O | String | | mobile | Mobile number of the consumer | O | Numeric | | pan | PAN number of the consumer | O | Alphanumeric | | start_date | Mandate start date | M | Date (YYYY-MM-DD) | | end_date | Mandate end date | M | Date (YYYY-MM-DD) | | amount | Mandate Amount | M | String, but should be a parseable positive Float/ Integer | | amount_rule | Rule of Amount. Permissible values FIXED, MAX. | M | Fixed
(FIXED or MAX) | | category_code | Category Codes. Permissible values API, B2B, CRED, MAND, EDU, PREM, PAYM, LONS, LONP, MF, OTH, SUB, TRE, TAX, ELEC, GAS, TELE, WAT. | M | Fixed (as per masters in description) | | frequency | Frequency of the Mandate. Permissible values one-time, adhoc, intra-day, daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, semi-annually, yearly, bi-monthly. | M | Fixed (one-time, adhoc, intra-day, daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, semi-annually, yearly, bi-monthly) | | authentication_mode | Mode of Mandate registration. Permissible values NETBANKING, DEBITCARD. | M | Fixed (NETBANKING, DEBITCARD) | | payee_account_number | Virtual Account Number where the collection is to be recorded. | CM, If a account number is passed, the money will settle in the mentioned account else money will be settled in master virtual account. | String |

item.description:
Recurring collections is way of facilitating collections from customers, individuals or businesses at pre-defined or regular intervals.

Some use-cases of recurring collections are.

*   **EMIs**: Lenders collecting repayments against a loan/credit line
*   **Premiums**: Insurers or businesses collecting payments towards premium
*   **Subscriptions**: Platforms collecting regular payments towards services
*   **Savings**: Platforms facilitating savings from customers at regular intervals
*   **Investments**: Platforms facilitating investments into various assets
    

Decentro powers recurring collections through 2 key modules.

*   [ENACH](https://docs.decentro.tech/#15011342-417f-4076-a62a-1b968068ec99): This is the NPCI powered bank account based mandate platform. The NACH stack is designed for larger ticket size and higher flexibility.
*   **UPI Auto-pay**: This is the NPCI powered UPI based platform. The UPI stack is designed for smaller ticket size and seamless registration. (Going live soon)

item.description:
This collection of APIs enables you to generate a digital invoice for your customers or buyers who need to pay money to your business or have already paid you offline (in cash or similar means). 

The invoice link once generated can then be sent by you over any digital channel like email, SMS, Whatsapp to the respective customer/buyer for making their payment via UPI. The same invoice can also be downloaded as a PDF by them or by your business team for processing or storing separately as suitable. 

You can also choose to add your own branded sub-domain to the link (URL) of the invoice. For getting that feature, please connect with us on hello@decentro.tech

request.description:
This callback is used by Decentro to trigger the status of a transaction undertaken through the [Penny Pull - Intent API](https://docs.decentro.tech/reference/payments-userverification-pennypull-createintent) to a pre-configured endpoint of the platform.

The platform can share the endpoint for triggering this callback before going live at [support@decentro.tech](https://mailto:support@decentro.tech).

Note:

- This callback only propagates the terminal transaction status.
- This callback is triggered when a transaction status moves to SUCCESS only and not in case of PENDING, FAILURE or EXPIRED.

item.description:
Decentro's Penny pull (reverse penny drop) capability broadly encompasses the below APIs.

- [Create Intent](https://docs.decentro.tech/reference/payments-userverification-pennypull-createintent): This API allows the platform to create a penny-pull payment link where the payer can pay using any UPI app on their mobile device.
    

- [Get Transaction Status](https://docs.decentro.tech/reference/payments-userverification-pennypull-gts): This API allows the platform to fetch the status of a penny pull-based transaction by passing the Decentro ID received while creating a penny pull intent.
    

- [Transaction status callback](https://docs.decentro.tech/reference/payments-userverification-pennypull-callback): This callback allows the platform to receive the status of a penny pull transaction on a pre-configured endpoint.
This page gives you an overview of Decentro's penny pull/reverse penny drop capabilities.

This module has APIs to enable your business to collect payments from anyone via multiple banking channels, such as Unified Payments Interface (UPI) and more.

This includes but is not limited to initiating a UPI collection request to a specific UPI app, validating a UPI ID (handle), and generating a payment link for UPI collections. Also, we enable your business to raise an invoice to another business or a customer directly via an API, and also generate payout links for anybody to receive money from your business.

The underlying partner banks that we have currently are.

*   Yes Bank (Live)
*   ICICI Bank (Live)
*   Axis Bank (Live)
    

And more in pipeline.

Decentro broadly has the below capabilities from a payments standpoint.

*   **Payouts**: Facilitate payouts to individuals or businesses using any of the supported modes (NEFT/IMPS/RTGS/UPI)
*   **Collections**: Facilitate collections from your customers or business partners using virtual accounts or UPI
*   **Mandates**: Facilitate recurring collections from individuals or businesses using ENACH or UPI Auto-pay based mandates

request.description:
This API is used to update the status of a Journal.

Here are how the status toggles work:

1.  The status of an ACTIVE Journal can be made either ON_HOLD or INACTIVE.
2.  The status of an ON_HOLD Journal can be made either ACTIVE or INACTIVE
3.  The status of an INACTIVE Journal can't be changed as it is a permanent status, revival from which is not allowed. Please be cautious and sure when making a Journal INACTIVE
    

| **Parameter** | **Description** | Optional (O)/ Mandatory (M)/ Conditionally mandatory (CM) | **Datatype** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| journal_urn | A valid Unique Reference Number associated with the Journal | M | String |
| reference_id | A unique ID assigned by the API user for this request. | M | String |
| purpose | Purpose of updating this Journal | M | String |
| consent | Consent of the API user | M | Boolean |
| status | Updated status of the Journal. Permissible values: ACTIVE, INACTIVE, ON_HOLD | M | String |

request.description:
This API is used for creating a Ledger Account belonging to the specified Active Journal in an Active status.

Important points:

1.  Attaching consumer details with a Ledger Account is optional.
2.  It is possible to have Ledger Accounts with different currencies within the same Journal.
3.  Only the Ledger Accounts with the same selected currencies can transact among each other.
    

| **Parameter** | **Description** | Optional (O)/ Mandatory (M)/ Conditionally mandatory (CM) | **Datatype** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| journal_urn | A valid Unique Reference Number associated with the Journal for which the Ledger Account is to be created | M | String |
| reference_id | A unique ID assigned by the API user for this request. | M | String |
| name | Name of the Ledger Account | M | String |
| consumer_reference_id | Unique Reference ID of a consumer associated with this Ledger Account | O | String |
| consumer_name | Name of the consumer associated with this Ledger Account | CM; if consumer_reference_id exists | String |
| currency | A valid currency code e.g. INR. Kindly use the [/currency](https://postman.decentro.tech/#a32b3761-a7f6-4011-a2f5-9e0367b24aa8) API to fetch the list of permissible currencies | M | String |
| purpose | Purpose of creating this Ledger Account | M | String |
| consent | Consent of the API user | M | Boolean |
| additional_parameters | Additional parameters to be attached with this Ledger Account | O | List; A maximum of 5 key-value pairs can be added within this list of JSON |
| key | Key for the additional parameter | CM, if the value parameter is present in the additional_parameters JSON | String |
| value | Value for the additional parameter | CM, if the key parameter is present in the additional_parameters JSON | String |

request.description:
This API is used to update the status of a Ledger Account belonging to the specified Active Journal.

Here are how the status toggles work:

1.  The status of an ACTIVE Ledger Account can be made either ON_HOLD or INACTIVE.
2.  The status of an ON_HOLD Ledger Account can be made either ACTIVE or INACTIVE
3.  The status of an INACTIVE Ledger Account can't be changed as it is a permanent status, revival from which is not allowed. Please be cautious and sure when making a Ledger Account INACTIVE
    

| **Parameter** | **Description** | Optional (O)/ Mandatory (M)/ Conditionally mandatory (CM) | **Datatype** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| journal_urn | A valid Unique Reference Number associated with the Journal | M | String |
| ledger_account_urn | A valid Unique Reference Number associated with the Ledger Account | M | String |
| reference_id | A unique ID assigned by the API user for this request. | M | String |
| purpose | Purpose of updating this Ledger Account | M | String |
| consent | Consent of the API user | M | Boolean |
| status | Updated status of the Ledger Account. Permissible values: ACTIVE, INACTIVE, ON_HOLD | M | String |

response.name: SUCCESS

request.description:
This API is used for fetching the Transaction statement of the specified Active Ledger Account belonging to the specified Active Journal.

This paginated API will respond with 10 entries per trigger with the most recently created Transaction on the top and the page parameter can be used to navigate the list accordingly.

| **Parameter** | **Description** | Optional (O)/ Mandatory (M)/ Conditionally mandatory (CM) | **Datatype** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| journal_urn | A valid Unique Reference Number associated with the Journal | M | String |
| ledger_account_urn | A valid Unique Reference Number associated with the Ledger Account | M | String |

response.name: SUCCESS

item.description: This folder contains APIs pertaining to Ledger Accounts.

item.name: Transaction

header.description: Decentro assigned client_id for the company
Decentro assigned client_secret for the company
Decentro assigned ledger module_secret for the company

variable.description: Unique Reference Number associated with the Journal

request.description:
This API is used for creating the following types of Transactions against the specified Active Journal. Transferring

1.  into a Ledger Account
2.  from a Ledger Account to another Ledger Account
3.  from a Ledger Account
    

Important points:

1.  Inter-Journal transfers are not supported.
2.  In case of a Ledger Account to Ledger Account transfer, only the Ledger Accounts with the same selected currencies can transact among each other.
    

| **Parameter** | **Description** | Optional (O)/ Mandatory (M)/ Conditionally mandatory (CM) | **Datatype** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| journal_urn | A valid Unique Reference Number associated with the Journal | M | String |
| reference_id | A unique ID assigned by the API user for this request. | M | String |
| purpose | Purpose of creating this Transaction | M | String |
| consent | Consent of the API user | M | Boolean |
| payer_ledger_account_urn | Unique Reference Number of the Payer Ledger Account | CM; Mandatory in case of an incoming or a Ledger Account to Ledger Account transfer | String |
| amount | Amount of the transaction | M | Parseable positive Int/Float |
| payee_ledger_account_urn | Unique Reference Number of the Payee Ledger Account | CM; Mandatory in case of an outgoing or a Ledger Account to Ledger Account transfer | String |
| status | Status of the Transaction | M | String |
| transaction_timestamp | Timestamp to be associated with the Transaction | M | String; Accepted formats: 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS' or 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.ms(6)' |
| additional_parameters | Additional parameters to be attached with this Journal | O | List; A maximum of 5 key-value pairs can be added within this list of JSON |
| key | Key for the additional parameter | CM, if the value parameter is present in the additional_parameters JSON | String |
| value | Value for the additional parameter | CM, if the key parameter is present in the additional_parameters JSON | String |

response.name: SUCCESS - Outgoing with additional_parameters

variable.description: Unique Reference Number associated with the Journal
Unique Reference Number associated with the Transaction

request.description:
This API is used to update the status of a Transaction belonging to the specified Active Journal.

Here are how the status toggles work:

1.  The status of a PENDING Transaction can be made either SUCCESS or FAILURE.
2.  The status of a SUCCESS or a FAILURE Transaction can't be changed as these are terminal statuses, implying that a Transaction can be updated only once.
    

Important points:

1.  The status of the Transaction can only be updated when the underlying Ledger Account(s) is/are in an ACTIVE state.
    

| **Parameter** | **Description** | Optional (O)/ Mandatory (M)/ Conditionally mandatory (CM) | **Datatype** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| journal_urn | A valid Unique Reference Number associated with the Journal | M | String |
| transaction_urn | A valid Unique Reference Number associated with the Transaction | M | String |
| reference_id | A unique ID assigned by the API user for this request. | M | String |
| purpose | Purpose of updating this Transaction | M | String |
| consent | Consent of the API user | M | Boolean |
| status | Updated status of the Transaction. Permissible values: SUCCESS, FAILURE | M | String |

response.name: SUCCESS

item.description:
Here you can use our APIs to perform actions like - 
- Search for and fetch the details of any bank using the IFSC code 
- Search for and fetch the details of any bank using the branch name
and more.

These APIs can be utilised by generating a token using your social login (Gmail or GitHub). 
To generate your token, [please click here](https://utils.decentro.tech/signin)

item.description:
These APIs can be utilised for fetching the list of our internal reference codes that are used from time to time for passing as parameters to various APIs. For example - 
- Bank codes 
- State codes 
- Merchant category codes etc. 
And many more
This is our common module with various utility APIs that you can hit free of charge based on your product usecase and business requirements.

All modules, submodules and APIs for our customers operating in India for financial integrations. 

For referring to all possible response codes and messages from our APIs, please refer to this list [here](https://docs.decentro.tech/#b8a8a159-4307-44c2-bf82-31d55787ecc8) below.
